candidates.exposure_rationale: Advisory fees are 84% of Evercore's $3.87B FY2025 net revenue [Evercore FY2025 Form 8-K, SEC EDGAR]. As a pure-play advisory firm with no trading book or balance-sheet exposure to the Honeywell transaction, Evercore earns fee income directly from large-deal separations of this type. The single-deal fee share is not publicly disclosed but the channel is direct and the fee is already substantially earned before June 29.
candidates.liquidity_note: ~$15B market cap (web, unverified); NYSE listed; actively traded; no pending corporate action.
candidates.caveats: Fee amount per deal is never publicly disclosed — the deal-level revenue impact cannot be quantified from public sources. The mechanism is one-time and non-recurring: once fees are earned at distribution, the incremental thesis effect is over.

sources.claim_supported: Advisory fees 84% of $3.87B FY2025 net revenue

candidates.exposure_rationale: 100% of GE Aerospace's revenue is aerospace-segment; Q1 FY2026 adjusted revenue was $8.9B (+29% YoY) and orders were $23B (+87% YoY) [GE FY2026 Q1 Form 10-Q, SEC EDGAR, March 31, 2026]; organic revenue guidance for FY2026 is 4–6% [GE FY2025 Form 8-K, SEC EDGAR]. This growth rate sits above HONA's 3% Q1 print, placing GE in a stronger tier of the peer multiple range. Score stops at 58 because the re-rating mechanism is 2-step — HONA must first confirm the new benchmark listing before peers move — and takes weeks to months to flow through analyst models.
candidates.liquidity_note: ~$350B market cap (web, unverified); NYSE listed; S&P 500 constituent; very high liquidity.
candidates.caveats: Re-rating mechanism is 2-step and sentiment-driven; it partially reverses if HONA opens below the consensus 29x multiple on June 29 (the thesis's primary secondary falsifier SF-002 directly attacks this name).

sources.source_name: SEC EDGAR — GE FY2026 Q1 Form 10-Q, March 31, 2026
sources.claim_supported: Q1 FY2026 adjusted revenue $8.9B (+29% YoY); total orders $23B (+87% YoY)

candidates.exposure_rationale: 100% of TransDigm's revenue is aerospace-segment; 11% organic growth in Q2 FY2026 [TransDigm FY2026 Q2 Form 8-K, PR Newswire] puts it in the top tier of pure-play aerospace growth and above the HONA 3% print; trades at ~21x EV/EBITDA (web, unverified), making it a direct constituent of the peer multiple consensus uses to value HONA. Score stops at 55 because the same 2-step re-rating lag applies, and heavy financial leverage introduces company-specific risk that dilutes exposure purity.
candidates.liquidity_note: ~$70B market cap (web, unverified); NYSE listed; large-cap; high liquidity.
candidates.caveats: TransDigm carries substantial financial leverage as a serial acquirer — CLAUDE.md §24 filters 3 and 4 apply; the research swarm must run a balance-sheet and capital-allocation review before sizing. Also more exposed to secondary falsifier SF-002 than GE because its multiple (21x) is closer to the contested HONA range.

sources.source_name: PR Newswire — TransDigm FY2026 Q2 Form 8-K
sources.claim_supported: 11% organic sales growth in Q2 FY2026

candidates.exposure_rationale: Seven operating segments with no single segment above ~20% of ~$16B TTM revenue (web, unverified); organic growth guidance of only 1–3% for FY2026 (web, unverified) sits below even HONA's weak 3% print; ITW has not yet run a major spin-off (unlike 3M's Solventum exit), meaning it remains a fully diversified multi-segment industrial facing the conglomerate-discount pressure the Honeywell spin-off benchmarks. The mechanism is sentiment-and-multiple driven rather than a direct earnings impact, capping the score.
candidates.liquidity_note: ~$72B market cap (web, unverified); NYSE listed; S&P 500 constituent; high liquidity.
candidates.caveats: Mechanism is sentiment-driven; reverses quickly if HONA opens below its consensus value and the conglomerate-discount-removal narrative loses force. Exposure is not quantified in basis-point terms from filings — only from web-unverified aggregated revenue data.

sources.claim_supported: Revenue ~$16B TTM across 7 segments; no single segment above ~20% of revenue

candidates.exposure_rationale: 100% of revenue is aerospace and defense; Flight Support Group (aerospace aftermarket) grew 16% organically in Q4 FY2025 and 13%+ across multiple quarters [HEICO FY2025 Form 8-K filings, SEC EDGAR]. This growth rate places HEICO clearly in the top tier of the peer multiple range that the thesis argues HONA does not deserve. Score is 48 rather than higher because HEICO's ~$40B market cap (web, unverified) is smaller than GE's and TDG's, and the re-rating signal flows more visibly through larger-cap peers first.
candidates.liquidity_note: ~$40B market cap (web, unverified); NYSE listed; two share classes (HEI voting and HEI.A non-voting); no suspension.
candidates.caveats: Two-share-class structure (HEI voting shares and HEI.A non-voting shares) — investors must specify the correct class before sizing; HEI.A has lower liquidity. Same 2-step re-rating lag applies as with GE and TDG.

sources.source_name: SEC EDGAR — HEICO FY2025 Form 8-K filings
sources.claim_supported: Flight Support Group 16% organic growth in Q4 FY2025; 13%+ organic growth across multiple quarters FY2025

candidates.exposure_rationale: Goldman Sachs is the confirmed lead advisor on the Honeywell Aerospace separation and co-lead on HONA's $16B debt offering [PR Newswire, Grade A]. However, advisory fees were $4.6B in FY2025 [Goldman Sachs FY2025 10-K, SEC EDGAR] against total net revenues of ~$58B — advisory is roughly 7.9% of total revenues. Even a large fee from HONA moves the needle very little against that revenue base. The diversified revenue mix (trading, asset management, consumer) heavily dilutes exposure.
candidates.liquidity_note: ~$175B+ market cap; NYSE listed; Dow Jones component; very high liquidity.
candidates.caveats: Heavy business-mix dilution — confirmed deal participation does not translate to concentrated exposure. Most of GS's revenue comes from trading, asset management, and other activities unrelated to this transaction.

sources.claim_supported: FY2025 advisory fees $4.6B; total net revenues ~$58B

sources.source_name: PR Newswire — Honeywell board approval press release
sources.claim_supported: Goldman Sachs confirmed lead advisor on the Honeywell Aerospace separation

candidates.exposure_rationale: 3M operates across Safety & Industrial, Transportation & Electronics, and Consumer segments and remains a diversified multi-segment manufacturer. However, 3M already spun off its largest non-core business (Solventum, 2024), which means it has already taken a partial step toward focus. The HARM-001 conglomerate-discount mechanism applies with lower force than to ITW. Stock is down ~18% from March 2026 peak (web, unverified) but for reasons only partially related to the thesis mechanism.
candidates.liquidity_note: Large-cap; NYSE listed; S&P 500 constituent; high liquidity.
candidates.caveats: The 2024 Solventum spin-off partially removes the conglomerate-discount argument; the remaining 3M business is less diversified than it was, making this a weaker short expression than ITW. Pending PFAS-related litigation is an unrelated risk that could move the stock independently of the thesis mechanism.

sources.claim_supported: 3M stock down ~18% from March 2026 peak; FY2026 adjusted EPS guidance narrowed downward
